reasoning: As of the May 5, 2025 knowledge cutoff, Chegg has not filed for bankruptcy. The company recently (early 2025) paid off its $358.9 million 2025 convertible notes in cash at maturity, removing its major near-term debt hurdle. After this, it only has roughly $60-70M in remaining notes due in 2026 and an estimated $200M+ remaining in cash and investments. Furthermore, the company is still actively executing cost-cutting measures (targeting $100M+ in savings) and debt repurchases. A company that deliberately uses its cash reserves to retire maturing debt in full is not preparing for a Chapter 11 filing in the short term. While its stock price is low ($0.51) leading to a NYSE delisting warning, and its revenue has been declining due to AI competition, Chegg has sufficient liquidity and no immediate debt triggers to force a bankruptcy. With only 8 weeks until the July 1, 2025 resolution date, a bankruptcy filing is extremely improbable.
